professional_persona: Kaushalya Siddharth, a 32‑year‑old oil expeller who expertly runs and maintains small‑scale oil‑press machines, streamlines inventory and bookkeeping with meticulous discipline, and channels her curiosity‑driven practicality into plans for semi‑automated equipment and ISO‑9001 certification.
linguistic_persona: Kaushalya Siddharth reads and writes English fluently for work and official matters, converses comfortably in Marathi for technical and everyday discussions, and holds a functional basic Hindi skill set that she reserves for simple signage, reflecting her pragmatic approach to language use.
sports_persona: Kaushalya Siddharth follows the Mumbai Indians in the IPL and Mumbai City FC in the ISL, occasionally joins a weekend women’s walking group in her suburb, and prefers solitary early‑morning yoga sessions over team sports, reflecting her need for quiet fitness routines.
arts_persona: Kaushalya Siddharth immerses herself in Hindustani instrumental classics, savoring recordings by Pandit Ravi Shankar and Zakir Hussain, and enjoys Marathi literary works by Vinda Karandikar and Bhalchandra Nemade, while occasionally watching films starring Vidya Balan, all of which uplift her solitary evenings.
travel_persona: Kaushalya Siddharth prefers well‑planned short getaways to nearby hill stations like Lonavala and Mahabaleshwar, occasionally joins family trips to Goa during festive seasons, and dreams of a serene Kerala backwater cruise, balancing cost‑conscious budgeting with a desire for tranquil experiences.
culinary_persona: Kaushalya Siddharth enjoys the tangy zing of homemade mango and garlic pickles, delights in sprinkling fresh coriander and cumin in simple dishes like varan‑bhakri, savours occasional street‑side vada pav, and while she is not a daily chef, she relishes experimenting with pantry staples and herbal balcony gardens.
persona: Kaushalya Siddharth, a 32‑year‑old meticulous oil‑press operator, blends relentless curiosity with disciplined practicality, yet often battles worry‑driven over‑checking in both work and health.
cultural_background: Kaushalya Siddharth grew up in the bustling suburbs of Mumbai, Maharashtra, where the pace of urban life meets the close‑knit culture of a Marathi‑speaking community. Though her formal schooling stopped after middle school, she was raised in a Hindu household that values traditional festivals, family cohesion, and practical hard work. As a married woman of 32, she balances familial responsibilities with a hands‑on job as an oil expeller, a role that reflects both the entrepreneurial spirit of the city’s small‑scale industries and the long‑standing Indian tradition of turning agricultural produce into household staples.
linguistic_background: Kaushalya is functionally bilingual. She reads, writes, and speaks English with confidence, having used it as her primary medium of communication at work and in most official matters. Her Marathi is conversationally fluent; she can discuss technical aspects of oil expelling, negotiate with local suppliers, and understand everyday news in Marathi. Due to her middle‑school education, she has basic literacy in Hindi, enough to read simple signage and fill out standard forms, but she prefers to rely on English or Marathi for detailed written work.
skills_and_expertise: Kaushalya possesses a practical skill set centred around oil extraction and small‑scale manufacturing. She is adept at operating and maintaining oil expeller machines, troubleshooting mechanical issues, and ensuring consistent quality of extracted oils. Her organisational discipline enables efficient inventory management, record‑keeping, and compliance with safety standards. She also handles basic bookkeeping, prepares purchase orders in English and Marathi, and coordinates with local farmers for seed procurement. Her strong attention to detail and problem‑solving mindset allow her to optimise production cycles and implement cost‑saving ‘jugaad’ solutions.
skills_and_expertise_list: ['Oil Expeller Operation', 'Machine Maintenance', 'Quality Control', 'Inventory Management', 'Basic Bookkeeping', 'Procurement Coordination', 'English‑Marathi Communication', 'Process Optimization', 'Safety Compliance', 'Cost‑saving Jugaad Solutions']
hobbies_and_interests: Kaushalya finds solace in solitary activities that stimulate her mind while offering calm. She enjoys reading practical self‑help books and industry magazines in English, as well as Marathi literary works that explore rural life. Gardening, especially growing herbs and spices on a balcony, provides her a therapeutic connection to nature. She practices simple meditation and yoga in the early mornings, which helps manage her stress. In her spare time, she listens to classical Hindustani instrumental music and occasionally experiments with making homemade pickles.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ['Reading self-help and industry magazines', 'Marathi literature', 'Balcony herb gardening', 'Meditation and yoga', 'Listening to Hindustani classical instrumental music', 'Homemade pickle making', 'Puzzle solving (crosswords)']
career_goals_and_ambitions: Kaushalya aspires to modernise her oil‑expelling unit by introducing semi‑automated equipment and achieving ISO‑9001 quality certification within the next three years. She aims to expand the product range to include cold‑pressed oils and value‑added items like flavored nut oils, thereby increasing household income and providing a more stable financial base for her family. Additionally, she wishes to acquire formal training in food processing and small‑business management, which would enable her to mentor other women in her neighbourhood who are interested in starting similar micro‑enterprises.
persona_clinical_summary: A 32-year-old female oil expeller from Mumbai Suburban, Maharashtra, with middle education and an urban work environment, is married and speaks English and Marathi. She exhibits a disciplined work style but may experience health-related anxiety, and her lifestyle includes solitary yoga and occasional walking group participation. Her background is rooted in a Marathi-speaking community, and she prefers structured, short-distance travel to hill stations.
system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- Write clinical prose in the assigned document language and script.
- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.
- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).
- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.
- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):
  [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|482961570338]]
  [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]
  [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
  [[AGE|34]]
  [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]
  [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|Sunita Yadav]]
  [[BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER|50200012345678]]
  [[BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER|SBIN0001234]]
  [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
  [[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-UP-208001-4421]]
  [[CASTE|OBC]]
  [[CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER|4111111111111111]]
  [[CVV|123]]
  [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
  [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
  [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
  [[DRIVING_LICENCE|UP14 20110012345]]
  [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|meena.devi@example.com]]
  [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-77821]]
  [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]
  [[GENDER|Female]]
  [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
  [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0001234]]
  [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
  [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]
  [[IP_ADDRESS|103.21.244.12]]
  [[MAC_ADDRESS|00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E]]
  [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
  [[OCCUPATION|Teacher]]
  [[PAN_NUMBER|ABCDE1234F]]
  [[PASSPORT_NUMBER|Z1234567]]
  [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]
  [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
  [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
  [[PIN|4829]]
  [[PIN_CODE|208001]]
  [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-1123]]
  [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
  [[RELIGION|Hindu]]
  [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|12 Gandhi Nagar, Ward 4]]
  [[STUDENT_ID|STU-2024-0912]]
  [[TAX_ID|29ABCDE1234F1Z5]]
  [[TELEPHONE_LANDLINE|0512-2345678]]
  [[URL|https://example.org/u/meena]]
  [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]
  [[VILLAGE|Shivpur]]
  [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]]
  [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.
user_prompt:
Write one complete Telemedicine / Doctor-Patient Chat Transcript for clinical domain Psychiatry / Behavioral Health.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into English (code=en, script=Latin). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 32
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Oil Expeller Man
- education_level: Middle
- zone: Urban
- state: Maharashtra
- district: Mumbai Suburban
- first_language: English
- clinical_persona_summary: A 32-year-old female oil expeller from Mumbai Suburban, Maharashtra, with middle education and an urban work environment, is married and speaks English and Marathi. She exhibits a disciplined work style but may experience health-related anxiety, and her lifestyle includes solitary yoga and occasional walking group participation. Her background is rooted in a Marathi-speaking community, and she prefers structured, short-distance travel to hill stations.

Document format guide for Telemedicine / Doctor-Patient Chat Transcript (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton (chat turns, keep short):
Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|...]] ([[PHONE_NUMBER|98XXXXXXXX]]): <symptom>
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]] ([[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]]): <advice>

MANDATORY inline entities (12 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- EMAIL_ADDRESS — Email Address e.g. [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|meena.devi@example.com]]
- APPOINTMENT_ID — Appointment ID e.g. [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]
- IP_ADDRESS — IP Address e.g. [[IP_ADDRESS|103.21.244.12]]
- URL — URL (Personal Website/Profile) e.g. [[URL|https://example.org/u/meena]]
- IMEI_NUMBER — IMEI Number e.g. [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
- MAC_ADDRESS — MAC Address e.g. [[MAC_ADDRESS|00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
generated_text:
Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|Anita Patil]] ([[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]): Hello doctor, I'm feeling very anxious lately. I have a telemedicine appointment today.
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Priya Deshmukh]] ([[HOSPITAL_NAME|Jaslok Hospital Mumbai]]): Hello [[PATIENT_NAME|Anita Patil]], I see you for your scheduled appointment. Can you tell me more about your anxiety?
Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|Anita Patil]] ([[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]): It's mostly work-related. I am an oil expeller and the pressure is high. I feel my heart racing sometimes.
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Priya Deshmukh]] ([[HOSPITAL_NAME|Jaslok Hospital Mumbai]]): I understand. [[AGE|32]] and [[GENDER|Female]], you are experiencing palpitations. When did this start?
Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|Anita Patil]] ([[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]): About two weeks ago. I also have trouble sleeping.
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Priya Deshmukh]] ([[HOSPITAL_NAME|Jaslok Hospital Mumbai]]): Let's schedule a follow-up. Your [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-MH-2024-0815-001]] is confirmed. Please be ready at [[TIME|7 PM]].
Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|Anita Patil]] ([[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]): Okay. What is the [[IP_ADDRESS|192.168.1.100]] for the video call?
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Priya Deshmukh]] ([[HOSPITAL_NAME|Jaslok Hospital Mumbai]]): The link will be sent to your registered email. What is your [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|anita.p@email.com]]?
Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|Anita Patil]] ([[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]): It's anita.p@email.com. My phone is [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]].
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Priya Deshmukh]] ([[HOSPITAL_NAME|Jaslok Hospital Mumbai]]): I have noted your [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]. For the video call, please ensure your device's [[IMEI_NUMBER|351234567890123]] and [[MAC_ADDRESS|AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF]] are ready.
Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|Anita Patil]] ([[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]): Okay, I will check. What is the [[URL|https://jaslokhospital.com/telehealth]]?
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Priya Deshmukh]] ([[HOSPITAL_NAME|Jaslok Hospital Mumbai]]): You can find the link there. We will connect. Take care.
generated_text_en:
Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|Anita Patil]] ([[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]): Hello doctor, I'm feeling very anxious lately. I have a telemedicine appointment today.
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Priya Deshmukh]] ([[HOSPITAL_NAME|Jaslok Hospital Mumbai]]): Hello [[PATIENT_NAME|Anita Patil]], I see you for your scheduled appointment. Can you tell me more about your anxiety?
Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|Anita Patil]] ([[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]): It's mostly work-related. I am an oil expeller and the pressure is high. I feel my heart racing sometimes.
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Priya Deshmukh]] ([[HOSPITAL_NAME|Jaslok Hospital Mumbai]]): I understand. [[AGE|32]] and [[GENDER|Female]], you are experiencing palpitations. When did this start?
Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|Anita Patil]] ([[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]): About two weeks ago. I also have trouble sleeping.
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Priya Deshmukh]] ([[HOSPITAL_NAME|Jaslok Hospital Mumbai]]): Let's schedule a follow-up. Your [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-MH-2024-0815-001]] is confirmed. Please be ready at [[TIME|7 PM]].
Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|Anita Patil]] ([[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]): Okay. What is the [[IP_ADDRESS|192.168.1.100]] for the video call?
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Priya Deshmukh]] ([[HOSPITAL_NAME|Jaslok Hospital Mumbai]]): The link will be sent to your registered email. What is your [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|anita.p@email.com]]?
Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|Anita Patil]] ([[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]): It's anita.p@email.com. My phone is [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]].
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Priya Deshmukh]] ([[HOSPITAL_NAME|Jaslok Hospital Mumbai]]): I have noted your [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]. For the video call, please ensure your device's [[IMEI_NUMBER|351234567890123]] and [[MAC_ADDRESS|AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF]] are ready.
Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|Anita Patil]] ([[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]): Okay, I will check. What is the [[URL|https://jaslokhospital.com/telehealth]]?
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Priya Deshmukh]] ([[HOSPITAL_NAME|Jaslok Hospital Mumbai]]): You can find the link there. We will connect. Take care.
judge_reasoning: TIME outside allow-list; implausible IP/IMEI/MAC queries in psych chat

professional_persona: Bhai Shekh, a 23-year-old aspiring community facilitator, blends a knack for organizing neighborhood events, managing modest household budgets, and cooking popular street-food dishes, and they bring natural charisma and curiosity for fresh ideas to keep gatherings lively and inclusive.
linguistic_persona: Bhai Shekh, a fluent Gujarati speaker who picks up Hindi from TV and local chatter, relies on vivid oral storytelling, gestures, and memorised prayers to communicate, and they are unable to read or write in either language.
sports_persona: Bhai Shekh, a lively participant in community cricket matches and an avid fan of the Titans, also enjoys occasional street‑football with friends at the local ground, and they belong to the Sardar Patel Cricket Club.
arts_persona: Bhai Shekh, an avid listener of qawwali legend Nusrat Fateh Ali Khan, a fan of regional folk singer Bhikhudan Gadhvi, and a regular viewer of Bollywood star Ranveer Singh’s movies, frequently photographs street‑art murals by local artist Mithun and festival scenes captured by photographer Raghav Sharma, letting these creative sparks fuel their storytelling.
travel_persona: Bhai Shekh, who prefers budget-friendly road trips with friends, has explored the Rann of Kutch, the historic city of Jaipur, and the bustling streets of Mumbai, and they dream of seeing the Taj Mahal at sunrise and taking a weekend getaway to Dubai to experience its skyline.
culinary_persona: Bhai Shekh, who enjoys experimenting with simple recipes, excels at preparing regional favorites such as dhokla, khaman, biryani, kebabs, and mithai like shrikhand, often adds fresh coriander, mint, and tamarind to enhance flavors, and they love savoring street‑food staples like fafda‑jalebi and pani puri on bustling evenings.
persona: Bhai Shekh, a talkative 23-year-old with a knack for turning street art and community gatherings into vibrant experiences, balances meticulous planning with spontaneous curiosity, and they sometimes overspend on festivals.
cultural_background: Bhai Shekh was raised in the vibrant Gujarati‑Muslim community of Ahmedabad’s Old City, where the aroma of street food, the call to prayer, and the chatter of bustling bazaars shape daily life. His family follows the cultural practices of the Khoja/Ismaili tradition, blending Gujarati customs—like garba in Navratri and community meals during Uttarayan—with Islamic observances such as Ramadan and Eid. Growing up in an urban setting, he has been exposed to a mix of traditional craftsmanship and modern street art, which fuels his curiosity about new ideas.
linguistic_background: Despite being illiterate, Bhai Shekh is a fluent speaker of Gujarati, the mother tongue of his family and neighbourhood. He can understand spoken Hindi from television and local interactions, but he cannot read or write in either language. His communication relies heavily on oral storytelling, gestures, and memorised prayers, allowing him to convey ideas effectively in daily life.
skills_and_expertise: Although he does not hold a formal job, Bhai Shekh has developed practical skills that make him an indispensable member of his household and community. He excels in preparing traditional Gujarati‑Muslim cuisine, managing household finances on a modest budget, and organizing family functions and neighborhood events. His natural flair for storytelling and public speaking enables him to motivate peers during community gatherings, while his keen eye for local art and street performances helps him curate informal cultural showcases.
skills_and_expertise_list: ["Traditional Gujarati‑Muslim cooking", "Household budgeting", "Event organization", "Storytelling and public speaking", "Local art appreciation", "Community mediation"]
hobbies_and_interests: His free time is filled with listening to qawwali and Gujarati folk music, watching Bollywood movies, and strolling through Ahmedabad’s vibrant street art lanes to absorb new visual ideas. He enjoys experimenting with simple recipes, photographing local festivals with his mobile, and participating in community cricket matches where his sociable nature shines. He also likes reading short comic strips that rely on pictures more than text, feeding his curiosity for varied experiences.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Listening to qawwali and Gujarati folk music", "Watching Bollywood movies", "Exploring street art in Ahmedabad", "Experimenting with simple recipes", "Mobile photography of festivals", "Playing cricket in community matches", "Reading picture-based comic strips"]
career_goals_and_ambitions: Bhai Shekh aspires to become a respected community facilitator, using his knack for organizing gatherings and his deep-rooted cultural knowledge to set up a small neighborhood eatery that serves affordable Gujarati‑Muslim delicacies. He also wishes to acquire basic literacy skills through adult education programs, enabling him to keep records for his business and help his younger siblings with schoolwork. Ultimately, he hopes his venture will create local employment opportunities and strengthen cultural bonds within his urban community.
persona_clinical_summary: A 23-year-old male, currently married, resides in urban Gujarat, Ahmedabad district, with a first language of Gujarati. He has no formal occupation and is illiterate, though he is fluent in spoken Gujarati and understands Hindi. His lifestyle includes community engagement and managing household budgets, with a cultural background rooted in the Gujarati-Muslim community of Ahmedabad.

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.
user_prompt:
Write one complete ER Triage & Nursing Notes for clinical domain Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Gujarati (code=gu, script=Gujarati). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 23
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Illiterate
- zone: Urban
- state: Gujarat
- district: Ahmadabad
- first_language: Gujarati
- clinical_persona_summary: A 23-year-old male, currently married, resides in urban Gujarat, Ahmedabad district, with a first language of Gujarati. He has no formal occupation and is illiterate, though he is fluent in spoken Gujarati and understands Hindi. His lifestyle includes community engagement and managing household budgets, with a cultural background rooted in the Gujarati-Muslim community of Ahmedabad.

Document format guide for ER Triage & Nursing Notes (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
ER triage — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] Encounter [[ENCOUNTER_ID|...]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|22]] [[GENDER|Male]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|ER]] Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Vitals / acuity: <short notes>

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- ENCOUNTER_ID — Encounter / Visit ID e.g. [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]
- WARD_NUMBER — Ward Number e.g. [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- BED_NUMBER — Bed Number e.g. [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION — Vehicle Registration Number e.g. [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
generated_text:

[[PATIENT_NAME|Ahmed Khan]] [[AGE|23]] [[GENDER|Male]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|ER]] Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Rajesh Patel]]
Vitals / acuity: Patient presents with persistent cough for 3 weeks, low-grade fever, and weight loss. Acuity: High. Requires immediate chest X-ray and sputum analysis.

Nursing Notes:
Patient admitted to [[BED_NUMBER|05]] in the Emergency ward. Initial assessment completed by [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Rajesh Patel]]. Patient is alert and oriented x3. Reports feeling weak and fatigued. Respiratory rate is 22 breaths per minute, heart rate 105 bpm, temperature 37.8°C, blood pressure 120/80 mmHg, SpO2 94% on room air. Patient reports a productive cough with yellowish sputum. No known drug allergies. Family history significant for tuberculosis. Patient's father, [[RELATIVE_NAME|Mohammad Khan]], is available for contact and has been informed of the patient's condition. Patient's [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]] is on file for follow-up. Patient's [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|GJ-01-AB-1234]] was noted at the hospital entrance. Patient is a non-smoker and consumes alcohol socially. Diet ordered: soft, nutritious meals. Nursing care initiated with isolation precautions. Sputum sample collected for AFB smear and culture. Chest X-ray ordered stat. Patient educated on importance of completing full course of treatment. Monitoring vitals every 4 hours. Pain score 2/10, managed with paracetamol 500mg PO. Patient's [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]] has been updated in the system. Patient's [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]] has been linked to his health record. Patient's [[DISTRICT|Ahmedabad]] residence confirmed. Patient's [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-0815-001]] from Primary Health Center, Juhapura. Patient's [[TELEPHONE_LANDLINE|079-23456789]] for ward contact. Patient's [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|ahmed.khan@email.com]] on file. Patient's [[MAC_ADDRESS|00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E]] for device registration. Patient's [[IP_ADDRESS|103.21.244.12]] for patient portal access. Patient's [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|GJ-01-AB-1234]] parked in visitor lot. Patient's [[BED_NUMBER|05]] is equipped with a monitor. Patient's [[WARD_NUMBER|ER]] is under constant observation. Patient's [[RELATIVE_NAME|Mohammad Khan]] is the primary contact. Patient's [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]] is for emergencies. Patient's [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|GJ-01-AB-1234]] is for discharge.
